chapter_context.summary: Asserts Israel's unique covenant relationship ('You only have I known') as the very reason for their severe punishment, using cause-and-effect questions to explain prophetic necessity, and prophesying the total destruction of Samaria's luxury and Bethel's altars.

outline.text: The Covenant Principle: Unique Privilege Brings Unique Accountability
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Covenant Principle: Unique Privilege Brings Unique Accountability (3:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: The Cause-and-Effect Questions: Prophetic Necessity and the Lion's Roar
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Cause-and-Effect Questions: Prophetic Necessity and the Lion's Roar (3:3-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: Summoning Foreign Witness to Samaria's Oppressions; The Metaphor of the Shepherd Saving Two Legs
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Summoning Foreign Witness to Samaria's Oppressions; The Metaphor of the Shepherd Saving Two Legs (3:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: The Demolition of Bethel's Altars and the Winter, Summer, and Ivory Houses
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Demolition of Bethel's Altars and the Winter, Summer, and Ivory Houses (3:13-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

key_themes.title: Social Justice and Equity
key_themes.description: Amos fiercely denounces religious ritual that exists alongside economic exploitation, emphasizing that true worship requires justice for the poor.

key_themes.title: The Danger of Spiritual Complacency
key_themes.description: The book warns that material prosperity can easily blind a nation to its spiritual and ethical decline, leading to swift judgment.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the indictments and visions of Chapter 3, how can you actively champion justice and care for the needy in your community today?
How can we guard ourselves against the kind of complacency and empty religious formalism that Amos rebuked in Samaria?

cultural_historical.title: The Reign of Jeroboam II
cultural_historical.text: Amos prophesied during the mid-8th century BC, a period of remarkable material prosperity, territory expansion, and luxurious stability for the Northern Kingdom under Jeroboam II, which unfortunately saw extreme wealth disparity and systemic social oppression.
